page: Microsoft Azure DS14 is an x86_64 architecture server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor running at 2.6 GHz. It features 16 physical cores and 16 vCPUs, with a dedicated CPU allocation managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The system is equipped with 112.0 GB of system memory, providing 7.0 GB of RAM per core. For local storage, the instance includes a 229 GB drive, while it contains no dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark results indicate a clear performance profile. Single-core performance is relatively weak, as shown by bottom-tier results in stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression tests. However, the server demonstrates average multi-core capabilities, with middle-tier performance in stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks also fall within the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the DS14 offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require top-tier single-threaded compute speeds. Tradeoffs include poor performance in large-scale LLM inference and weak single-core execution. It is well-suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and moderate multi-threaded processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure DS14 is an x86_64 server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 112.0 GB of RAM and 229 GB of local storage. Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance delivers a balanced hardware profile with 7.0 GB of memory per core. Benchmark data reveals weak single-core performance across stress-ng and Geekbench tests, contrasted by average multi-core and memory bandwidth capabilities. The server performs reliably in multi-threaded tasks, database operations, and Redis caching, but struggles with demanding single-threaded applications and large-scale LLM inference. Qualitatively, the DS14 offers a solid resource density for memory-heavy workloads that can tolerate moderate CPU speeds. It is best suited for database hosting, web serving, and general-purpose enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure DS14 is an x86_64 server with 16 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and 229 GB local storage, delivering average multi-core performance for database and general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure DS14 features 16 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 112 GB RAM, and 229 GB local storage, optimized for database and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-dense virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors and local storage for balanced multi-threaded workloads and database operations.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL vCPUs running at 2.6 GHz
112.0 GB of system memory providing 7.0 GB per core
Average multi-core and memory bandwidth performance
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and web serving
Weak single-core performance limits highly sequential workloads
